title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Commissioner Weston and Superintendent Corgherget discuss the investigation into Elena Marshall’s death. Corgherget notes that a letter signed "J.N." found in Elena's room was sent by a young man in China, a person previously mentioned by Miss Bruce. Weston considers the possibility of drug trafficking and recalls a connection to Reverend Stephen Lane, who entered a mental institution the previous Easter. Weston links this to two unsolved cases in Surrey: the death of a young woman near Bagshot and "The Mysterious Murder in the Wild Woods."

Meanwhile, Hercule Poirot sits on a cliff overlooking Elphiston Bay, reviewing a collection of disparate clues, including Chanel No. 5 perfume, nail clippers, a pipe fragment, a green calendar, a girl's watch, and drainage water. He seeks to connect these facts to confirm the presence of evil on the island.

Inspector Corgherget approaches Poirot to discuss unsolved strangulation cases. He reveals details regarding the death of Alice Corigan, who was found strangled in Caislin's Marsh. This case is geographically linked to the death of Nellie Parsons, a maid strangled in Marybush Wood, both of which are near Whitecliff, where Mr. Lennon serves as vicar. Although Alice Corigan's husband, Edward, was initially a suspect due to her life insurance, he was exonerated by a solid alibi involving a train journey and a meeting at the Scone Tea House. The police believe the same killer murdered both women, but the perpetrator was never caught. Poirot concludes by stating his intention to use a small, inconsequential lie as a test to expose a larger pattern of deception.
